title: Formula: single-project bead triage
description:
## Problem

No repeatable workflow for auditing/triaging a project's open beads. Agents currently do N individual bd show calls (burns context) and have no structured process for consolidation, reprioritization, or quality improvement.

## Proposed Solution

Create a .formula.toml for single-project bead triage with steps:

1. **Snapshot** - bd export to get full bead data in one shot (not N bd shows)
2. **Hygiene** - bd lint + bd stale + bd orphans as automated passes
3. **Cluster** - LLM-driven grouping by theme/area
4. **Consolidate** - Find duplicates, bd supersede losers into winners
5. **Quality pass** - Ensure descriptions have problem statements + acceptance criteria, wire parent/child to epics
6. **Reprioritize** - Rerank relative to each other with clear criteria (P1=blocking, P2=next, P3=backlog, P4=someday)
7. **Gate: human** - Review proposed changes before execution
8. **Execute** - Apply merges, reparents, priority changes, dep adds
9. **Summary** - Report what changed

## Acceptance Criteria

- Formula exists and can be poured as a wisp: bd mol wisp bead-triage
- bd ready sequences the steps correctly
- Human gate blocks execution until user approves
- Uses bd export instead of individual bd shows for efficiency
notes:
2026-04-14: Bumped to P0. After a full day of dogfooding across 15 projects, triage is the critical gap. Multiple sessions today produced rich transcripts about beads audits, MEOW, NDI, feature discovery, what worked and what didn't - these are high-value inputs for the triage formula design. Search cass for 2026-04-14 sessions in bt, cass, .obs, cnvs, tpane workspaces for context.
Live dogfood happening right now: cass session b933 is running a manual bead audit - exactly the workflow this formula would automate.

title: bd export emits metadata lines that break downstream JSON consumers
description:
## Problem

`bd export` emits 12 metadata/config lines at the end of the JSONL output with a different schema (`_type`, `key`, `value` fields - no `status`). Any downstream consumer doing `json.loads(line)["status"]` will crash with KeyError.

This is a data contract problem - it'll bite any tool consuming the export.

## Discovered By

Agent feedback during a `bd export` + Python analysis workflow.

## Proposed Solutions

1. **Document the metadata lines** in export format docs
2. **Add `--issues-only` flag** to filter out non-issue lines (preferred)
3. Both - document AND add the flag

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Downstream consumers can reliably parse export output without unexpected schema changes
- [ ] Either metadata is excluded by default, or there's a flag to exclude it
- [ ] Format documented in TEXT_FORMATS.md or similar
notes: GitHub issue: gastownhall/beads#3271. Fix: add _type:issue to IssueWithCounts marshal in export.go ~line 166. --no-memories already exists but isn't the default.
close_reason: PR #3272 submitted, fix cherry-picked to local main, bd rebuilt

title: llms-full.txt missing post-1.0 features: swarm, convoy, cook, MEOW, NDI, wisp lifecycle
description:
## Problem

llms-full.txt at website/static/llms-full.txt is a v1.0.0 snapshot that's missing significant implemented features. Agents reading this file (its stated purpose) conclude these features don't exist.

## Missing from llms-full.txt (verified present in Go source and versioned docs)

**Completely absent:**
- bd swarm (parallel execution from epic DAG)
- Convoy formula type (multi-agent coordination)
- bd cook (formula compilation, compile-time vs runtime modes)
- MEOW terminology (Molecular Expression of Work hierarchy)
- NDI (Nondeterministic Idempotence design philosophy)
- Formula inheritance (extends field)
- Formula loops/conditions/foreach (LoopSpec, condition field)
- Compile-time vs runtime variable substitution

**Partially documented (basic only, missing advanced):**
- Wisp lifecycle: squash, burn, promote, gc documented in versioned_docs but not in llms-full.txt (only bd wisp cleanup/delete)
- external_projects config for cross-project deps (only external: prefix mentioned)
- Federation (referenced but linked to external files, not inline)
- Bond operations (mol bond with phase control, conditional bonds)
- Distill (extracting proto from organic work)

## Source of truth

- Go source: internal/formula/types.go (~848 lines) has full implementation
- Versioned docs: website/versioned_docs/version-1.0.0/workflows/*.md have more detail
- Plugin resources: beads-marketplace skill resources (CHEMISTRY_PATTERNS.md, MOLECULES.md) have the most complete docs

## Fix

Regenerate llms-full.txt to include all workflow docs content, or add the missing sections. The file should match what the versioned docs and Go source actually implement.

## Impact

Users relying on llms-full.txt for LLM context (its designed purpose) miss 65%+ of the workflow system. This directly causes underadoption of formulas, molecules, wisps, and gates.
close_reason: Filed as gastownhall/beads#3244, investigated generation script - source docs need content updates, not script re-run

title: Beads feature discovery gap: agents don't know about gates, human system, formulas, and other power features
description:
## Problem

Beads has significant power features that agents (and the user) don't know exist or don't understand well enough to use:
- **Gate system** (`bd gate`): blocking coordination with human, timer, CI, PR gates
- **Human system** (`bd human`): advisory labeling (often confused with blocking)
- **Formula/molecule system**: workflow templates, SOPs, structured multi-step processes
- **Ship/export system**: cross-project capability contracts
- **Rich bead formatting**: full markdown, checklists, session setup instructions

The current beads-onboard skill teaches basic CRUD but doesn't cover these systems. The global PRIME.md has workflow basics but nothing about gates, formulas, or the human system. Agents create flat, shallow beads because they don't know what's possible.

## Two-Fold Output Required

### 1. Plugin/Harness Packaging (~/System/marketplace)
- Adapt beads-onboard skill to teach the full feature set
- Add skills/hooks for gate awareness, human-in-the-loop workflows
- Include global PRIME.md management (the complementary model for Tasks/beads/memory)
- Make this installable and transferable (Windows -> Mac)

### 2. Frontend Context (bt/beadstui)
- bt needs to surface gates, human flags, and blocking status in the TUI
- Custom CLI commands or views for the cross-project system
- Visual distinction for gated vs human-flagged vs blocked issues

## Root Cause

The gap isn't in beads itself - the features exist. The gap is in the teaching/convention layer:
- beads-onboard doesn't cover gates, formulas, human system
- Global PRIME.md doesn't mention gates or formulas
- Agent instructions (CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md injections) are minimal
- No 'feature discovery' mechanism - agents can't learn what they don't know exists
- Bead quality suffers because agents don't know rich formatting is possible/expected
notes:
## Feature Catalog

103 bd subcommands audited. 22 KNOWN (21%), 14 PARTIAL (14%), 67 UNKNOWN (65%).

**Critical confusions (Tier 1):**
1. **human vs gate**: `bd human` is advisory (adds label, does NOT block from `bd ready`). `bd gate` with human type is the actual blocking mechanism, created through formulas. Agents and user actively confuse these.
2. **Markdown in fields**: description/notes/design support full markdown (checklists, headers, code blocks). Nothing teaches agents to use structured markdown. All beads get flat text.
3. **Gate/formula/molecule stack invisible**: Two lines in PRIME.md. Entire async coordination, workflow templating, and work execution systems unknown to agents.

**Major gaps (Tier 2):** bd query (full boolean query language), bd swarm (parallel work coordination), bd merge-slot (serialization), bd ship (cross-project capability publishing), bd set-state/state (operational state), bd batch (bulk perf), bd kv (cross-session persistence).

**Moderate gaps (Tier 3):** bd graph (rich visualization), bd find-duplicates, bd comments, bd epic, bd promote, integrations (GitHub/Jira/Linear/GitLab/ADO/Notion), bd worktree, bd vc/branch/diff.

Full catalog: `docs/audits/bd-0il-feature-catalog.md`

## External Context

Research covered: gastownhall.ai, Yegge Medium articles (5), Dolt blog (3), GitHub repo, community tools (40+).

**Core positioning:** Beads is external working memory for agents, not a TODO list. Solves "50 First Dates" inter-session amnesia.

**MEOW stack:** beads -> epics -> molecules -> protomolecules -> formulas. Most agents use only layers 1-2.

**Yegge best practices:** File everything >2min, one task per session, 5 review iterations, bd doctor daily, keep <200 issues, short prefixes. "Desire paths design" - 100+ commands built by reverse-engineering agent expectations.

**Dolt patterns:** Cell-level merge, embedded single-writer with backoff, shared server mode, auto-push with 5min debounce. Concurrent access tested safe.

**Community:** 40+ tools (TUIs, web, editor extensions, AI orchestration). Three workflow patterns: prompt-first (reactive), issue-first (planned), hybrid. Power users: custom types, database redirects, label tiering, --stealth mode.

**Known issues:** bd update --defer may fail (#3233), list silently truncates at 50 (#3212), formula gates reject custom types (#3213).

Full research: `docs/audits/bd-0il-external-context.md`

## bt Angle

**P0 Must Have:**
1. Gate status in issue list/detail - show gate type, await_id, blocked-by info. Cross-project bead gates in global mode.
2. Human flag visual distinction - separate advisory badge from gate-blocked indicator. Makes the confusion visible.
3. Rich markdown rendering - bt has pkg/ui/markdown.go, ensure all fields render properly.

**P1 Should Have:**
- BQL/query integration, swarm wave visualization, ship/provides capability graph (global mode unique), stale/overdue highlighting, operational state indicators, epic progress, defer/undefer visual treatment.

**P2 Nice to Have:**
- Formula browser, merge slot indicator, KV viewer, duplicate detection, wisp toggle, version timeline, integration sync status.

**Global mode unique opportunities (bt-only views):**
- Cross-project gate map (bead gates spanning projects)
- Capability graph (export/provides/external labels aggregated)
- Cross-project state dashboard (dimension:value matrix)
- Project health heatmap

**Data strategy:** Extend model.Issue with await_type/await_id/mol_type/ephemeral fields. Parse state labels as structured badges. Shell out to bd for writes and formula/swarm data.

Connected beads: bt-c69c (human/gate surfacing), bt-8f34 (project registry).

Full analysis: `docs/audits/bd-0il-bt-angle.md`

## mkt Angle

**Three-layer teaching architecture fix:**
1. PRIME.md (awareness): What exists, when to reach for it. Max ~2k tokens.
2. Skills (depth): On-demand detailed guidance. New: /beads-gates, /beads-workflows.
3. Hooks (enforcement): Catch misuse in real-time. Critical: human/gate confusion guard.

**Phase 1 - Fix critical confusions (immediate):**
- Rewrite PRIME.md Human Decisions section with gate disambiguation (+20 tokens net)
- Add markdown-in-fields pointer to Creating & Updating (+40 tokens)
- Add "Beyond the Basics" awareness table - 9 feature groups discoverable via skill (+150 tokens)
- PostToolUse hook on `bd human <id>` - 50 token correction at moment of confusion
- New /beads-gates skill (~400 words) with decision framework

**Phase 2 - Surface power features (next release):**
- New /beads-workflows skill (~500 words) - formula/molecule/wisp entry ramp
- PRIME.md: bd query one-liner, bd batch mention, bd bootstrap in error table, bd undefer
- Hooks: rich description nudge, batch optimization hint
- beads-onboard v5: gate interview question, markdown templates, query examples, integration detection

**Phase 3 - Complete coverage (long-tail):**
- beads-onboard reference.md: find-duplicates, comments, epic close-eligible, graph
- Existing skill resource updates: history, set-state, integration commands
- PRIME.md additions only if user demand emerges

**Token budget:** PRIME.md stays under 2k through all phases. Phase 1: +210 tokens (14% growth). Phase 2: +75 more. Phase 3: +50 more.

**Key design decision:** Depth goes into skills (free when not needed), enforcement into hooks (fires at confusion point), PRIME.md stays lean as the awareness/discovery layer.

Full analysis: `docs/audits/bd-0il-mkt-angle.md`

## Dogfooding Notes

**What worked about using beads as the output destination:**
- The notes field handled the dense synthesis well - it forced compression of 4 long docs into actionable summaries
- Pointing back to full docs ("see docs/audits/bd-0il-*.md") is the right pattern - beads as index, .md as archive
- The structured sections (##) are scannable in bd show output
- Using --design for overflow would work but wasnt needed here

**What didnt work / friction:**
- The notes field has no preview - you write blind and hope the markdown renders. bd show --json wraps it in JSON escaping which makes it hard to verify
- Shell quoting for multiline markdown with backticks, pipes, and special chars is painful. Had to be careful with single quotes and escaping
- No way to append to notes incrementally - each update replaces the whole field. For phased work like this, youd want bd note (append) semantics
- Character/token limits on the notes field arent documented - unclear how much is too much

**Recommendation for marketplace training:**
- Teach agents to use notes for dense synthesis, .md files for full analysis, description for the problem statement
- The dual delivery pattern (docs + bead notes) works well as a deliberate dogfooding exercise
- For research tasks specifically, the bead becomes a table of contents pointing to detailed docs - not a replacement for them
- bd note (append) would be better than bd update --notes for incremental research delivery

title: Epic: Cross-project beads operating system
description:
A system where beads serves as the global cross-project issue tracker and communication layer for all projects in the user's operating system. Agents autonomously file beads in the right project (not just their own), link related work across projects with paired IDs (same suffix, different prefix - e.g. bt-zsy8/bd-zsy8), and the shared Dolt server provides the unified data layer.

Goals:
- Cross-project filing: any session can create beads in any project, with provenance tracking
- Paired ID linking: same-ID-suffix pattern for related cross-project work, with a formal relationship type
- Global visibility: bt (or any consumer) can query across all project databases on the shared server
- Portability: the system exports/installs cleanly across machines (Windows -> Mac)
- Agent conventions: prime.md/AGENTS.md instructions teach agents the cross-project workflow
- Safety: no raw SQL against shared server, only bd commands within project directories

This is the umbrella epic for the existing cross-project beads. The shared Dolt server is already the infrastructure backbone - this epic tracks building the conventions, tooling, and agent instructions on top of it.
close_reason: Migrated to bt project as bt-ushd. Children also migrated.

title: Dolt auto-push fails with 'no common ancestor' on beads repo
description: bd create/update triggers dolt auto-push, which fails with: Error 1105 (HY000): unknown push error; no common ancestor. This happens consistently in the beads project's own Dolt remote. Beads still get created locally but never sync to remote. Needs investigation: is the remote initialized correctly? Was there a force-push or reset that broke the ancestry chain?
close_reason: Not a bug for this repo: beads Dolt DB is local-only (no remote configured). Auto-push correctly fails with 'remote not found'. The original 'no common ancestor' error was likely transient during shared server migration. Issues are created and stored locally as intended.

title: Fix bd export to include comment bodies (upstream PR)
description: bd export includes comment_count but not comment text. The comments array is always empty in exported JSONL. This blocks migration to shared server since bd export/import round-trip loses all comment history. Root cause: export.go:142 calls store.GetCommentCounts() but never calls store.GetCommentsForIssues() to populate the Comments field. Fix: add GetCommentsForIssues() call alongside existing GetLabelsForIssues() and GetDependencyRecordsForIssues() calls, attach to each issue before marshaling. Filed upstream: gastownhall/beads#3007. Target: standalone PR, smallest of our three upstream contributions. Must be merged before migrating any repos with comment history.
close_reason: Already fixed upstream: PR #3019 and #3050 merged. Issue #3007 closed 2026-04-05.

title: bd repo sync needs Dolt-native cross-repo hydration
description: bd repo sync reads from issues.jsonl but beads is now Dolt-native. Three problems discovered when wiring .obs as a hub with 9 spoke repos: (1) Most JSONL files have stale schema - comment.id is numeric but current schema expects string, causing parse failures. (2) Repos initialized after Dolt migration have no issues.jsonl at all (tpane, marketplace, lil world). (3) Sync is one-directional snapshot, not live - imported issues are frozen copies with no back-propagation. Need either: Dolt-native sync (spoke DBs as remotes), a query layer that reads live from spoke Dolt DBs, or at minimum bd export that auto-refreshes JSONL before sync. This blocks the global beads vision (sms-ncb in .obs).
notes:
## Implementation Plan

Complete plan at: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-03-dolt-native-repo-sync.md

## Session Instructions

1. Create a feature branch: git checkout -b feat/dolt-native-repo-sync
2. Read the plan doc above - it has exact code, file paths, line numbers, and commit messages
3. Execute tasks 0-5 in order. Task 0 is a gate test - do not proceed if it fails.
4. Target: upstream PR to steveyegge/beads. Follow CONTRIBUTING.md guidelines.
5. PR should reference this as fixing the three problems in the description.
6. Separately, open an issue for prefix-scoped queries in shared server mode (bd list shows cross-prefix issues when multiple projects share one database - should auto-filter by configured prefix).

## Key Context

- Pulled to v1.0.0 (234da631) before planning. Plan is written against current main.
- Commit 543e1943 added flock to embeddeddolt.New() - our OpenExisting follows the same pattern.
- PR #2827 (remote URL multi-repo hydration) is the precedent for scope/style.
- One PR for all changes (OpenExisting + sync refactor + comment.id tolerance + help text + tests). They are tightly coupled - the comment.id fix is for the JSONL fallback path of the new behavior.
- Must add non-CGO stub for OpenExisting in store_stub.go.
- gh auth: use sms account (seanmartinsmith) for the PR.
close_reason: Migrated to bt project as bt-koz8

title: Link beads to originating session transcripts
description:
## Why

When a bead is created or modified, there's no record of which session did the work. To understand WHY a decision was made, you have to search cass for the bead ID and hope for a hit. With autonomous agents this becomes critical - the transcript IS the debug log.

Paired with: cass-fjip (cass project tracks the consumer side)

## What

Add session_id metadata to beads. Capture automatically:
- On bd create: store the current session ID (from cass whoami or CLAUDE_SESSION_ID env var)
- On bd close: store the closing session ID  
- On bd update with --notes/--description/--design: store the modifying session ID

Store as a session_history array on the bead: [{action: 'created', session_id: '...', timestamp: '...'}]

Surface with bd show: 'Created in session abc-123 (cass transcript <path>)'

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] bd create records session_id in bead metadata
- [ ] bd close records closing session_id
- [ ] bd show displays session provenance with transcript path
- [ ] Works when session ID is unavailable (graceful fallback)

title: Fix bd list --json truncation indicator (GH#3280)
description:
## Problem

bd list defaults to 50 results. The truncation hint goes to stderr via printTruncationHint (list_output.go:21), which agents using --json never see. They assume the JSON array contains all results.

## Fix

Add truncation metadata to JSON output mode. Either a _meta object or wrap the array response with truncated/limit fields. The stderr hint already has the right logic - needs a JSON-mode equivalent.

## Refs

- gastownhall/beads#3280
- list_output.go:17-22 (printTruncationHint)
- list.go:1022 (default limit 50)
close_reason: Investigated - not bugs. GH#3280 is solvable agent-side with --limit 0, GH#3279 is Cobra flag semantics working as designed. Comments posted upstream.

title: Draft and post comment on GH#3008 (shared-db dogfooding insights)
description:
## Context

GH#3008 is about bd list not auto-scoping by prefix in shared-database mode. maphew triaged it Apr 14, Pewejekubam confirmed the separate-database workaround works. We have novel insights to contribute:

- Running shared-server across 15 projects with separate databases per project
- Built bt (beadstui) for cross-project visibility via global mode
- Looking into gastown/gascity next
- bd list 50-item cap compounds the discoverability problem

## Task

Draft a comment and post it. Use casual dev voice per CLAUDE.md GitHub comment style. Verify draft with user before posting.

## Refs

- gastownhall/beads#3008
- bd-0i9 (related: bd list truncation confusion)

title: bd close with multiple IDs and --reason applies last reason to all issues
description:
## Bug

When closing multiple issues in one command with per-issue --reason flags, bd applies the LAST --reason to ALL issues instead of matching each reason to its preceding ID.

### Reproduction

```bash
bd close cass-nva --reason='reason A' cass-4r6 --reason='reason B' cass-jqv --reason='reason C'
```

**Expected:** Each issue gets its own reason (nva=A, 4r6=B, jqv=C).
**Actual:** All three get reason C.

### Evidence

From cass session 2026-04-14 (session b93322bc). Output was:
```
Closed cass-nva: reason C
Closed cass-4r6: reason C
Closed cass-jqv: reason C
```

### Impact

Close reasons are important for searchability and context recovery. When batch-closing, users lose the per-issue close context. Workaround is to close one at a time.

### Likely cause

The arg parser probably collects all --reason flags into a single value (last-wins) rather than associating each --reason with the preceding positional ID.
notes: GH#3279 filed upstream. PR tomorrow: fix cobra arg parsing to associate --reason with preceding positional ID.
close_reason: Investigated - not bugs. GH#3280 is solvable agent-side with --limit 0, GH#3279 is Cobra flag semantics working as designed. Comments posted upstream.

title: No example formulas ship with beads - zero adoption bootstrap
description:
## Problem

bd formula list returns 'No formulas found' on a fresh install. The formula system is powerful but there are zero .formula.toml files shipped with beads. All examples exist only as doc snippets. Users have no starting point.

## Suggestion

Ship 3-5 starter formulas in a default location:
- feature-workflow.formula.toml (the basic design->implement->review->merge)
- release.formula.toml (version bump->test->tag->publish)
- quick-check.formula.toml (lint->test->build, designed as wisp)

These serve as both documentation-by-example and immediately useful workflows.

## Connected

bd-bfm (llms-full.txt gaps) - even if docs are fixed, users need something to pour/wisp on day one.
close_reason: Filed as gastownhall/beads#3246

title: Audit: GETTING_STARTED_ANALYSIS.md flags docs 3x longer than needed - act on it
description:
## Problem

docs/GETTING_STARTED_ANALYSIS.md is an internal audit that found the getting-started docs are ~3x longer than needed. The finding exists but was never acted on. New users (and agents) face unnecessarily verbose onboarding.

## Connected

bd-bfm (llms-full.txt gaps) - the LLM docs are both incomplete AND verbose in the parts they do cover. Both issues contribute to poor feature adoption.
close_reason: Filed as gastownhall/beads#3245

title: Session author provenance: track which session/project filed a bead
description: When a session from project A creates a bead in project B (cross-project filing), the target project should know where it came from. CreatedBy field exists but tracks user, not session/project origin. Need: session ID, source workspace/project, and optionally the source agent type stored on the issue. This enables bt to show 'filed by bt session' or 'filed from beads project' in the TUI, and supports the notification hook (bd-e6p).
close_reason: Migrated to bt project as bt-2cvx

title: bd human is advisory-only - need actual blocking mechanism for human decisions
description:
## Problem

`bd human` is advisory-only (adds a label, doesn't block). `bd gate await:human` is the actual blocking mechanism but it's only creatable through the formula/molecule system - there's no ad-hoc way to say 'this issue needs human input before work continues' and have it actually block from `bd ready`.

## Current State

Two independent human-in-the-loop mechanisms exist in beads:

### bd human (Advisory)
- Adds `human` label to an issue
- `bd human list` shows flagged issues
- `bd human respond <id> -r 'answer'` adds comment + closes
- `bd human dismiss <id>` closes without response
- **Does NOT block** - issue still shows in `bd ready`

### bd gate await:human (Blocking)
- Creates a gate issue (type: `gate`, await_type: `human`)
- Gate blocks downstream steps via `DepBlocks` dependency
- Blocked steps excluded from `bd ready` until gate resolves
- Resolved via `bd gate resolve <id>` (manual only)
- Has waiter/notification support
- **Only creatable through formulas** (`[steps.gate]` in `.formula.toml`)

### Other gate types (context)
- `timer`: auto-resolves after timeout
- `gh:run`: waits for GitHub Actions CI
- `gh:pr`: waits for PR merge
- `bead`: cross-rig coordination (currently disabled)

## Gap

No simple CLI path to create a blocking human gate on an arbitrary issue outside the formula system. Something like:
```bash
bd gate create --type=human --blocks=bd-xxx --reason='Need design decision on X'
```

This is critical for the cross-project OS (bd-mh6) where agents in any project need to pause and surface decisions to the human operator.

## Key Files
- `cmd/bd/gate.go:18-795` - Gate commands
- `cmd/bd/human.go:14-400` - Human label commands
- `cmd/bd/cook.go:478-646` - Gate creation in formulas
- `internal/storage/issueops/blocked.go:52-99` - Blocking logic
- `internal/storage/issueops/ready_work.go:53` - Ready filtering excludes gates
- `internal/types/types.go:91-95` - AwaitType, AwaitID, Timeout, Waiters fields
notes:
## Session Setup

When picking up this bead, orient yourself first:

### Required Reading
1. `bd gate --help` and all subcommands (`list`, `show`, `resolve`, `check`, `discover`)
2. `bd human --help` and subcommands (`list`, `respond`, `dismiss`, `stats`)
3. `cmd/bd/cook.go:478-646` - how formulas create gates (the pattern to generalize)
4. `website/docs/workflows/gates.md` - upstream docs on gate system

### Suggested Task Breakdown
- [ ] Read gate creation in cook.go to understand the pattern
- [ ] Prototype: can you create a gate issue manually via `bd create --type=gate` + set AwaitType?
- [ ] If not, what's the minimum change to support ad-hoc gate creation?
- [ ] Design CLI: `bd gate create --type=human --blocks=<id>` vs extending `bd human <id> --block`
- [ ] Consider: should this be an upstream PR or a local extension?
- [ ] Test: create gate, verify blocked issue disappears from `bd ready`, resolve gate, verify it reappears

### Context
- Parent epic: bd-mh6 (Cross-project beads OS)
- Related: bd-9lt (session author provenance), bd-cxd (cross-project filing)
- bt session needs this for TUI integration - bt-c69c tracks exposing human commands in beadstui
- The bt session (ff42a7e4) incorrectly described bd human as blocking - this bead documents the actual behavior
close_reason: Filed as gastownhall/beads#3256, PR gastownhall/beads#3257

title: Investigate upstream reasoning for 'do NOT use TaskCreate/MEMORY.md' in prime fallback
description:
The hardcoded fallback in cmd/bd/prime.go (lines ~310, ~428) and internal/templates/agents/defaults/beads-section-minimal.md says 'Prohibited: Do NOT use TodoWrite, TaskCreate' and 'Do NOT use MEMORY.md'. Our global PRIME.md override at $APPDATA/beads/PRIME.md has the complementary model (Tasks for in-session, beads for cross-session; bd remember for project-scoped, MEMORY.md for cross-project).

Action items:
1. Research whether there's a specific upstream reason for the blanket prohibition (performance, confusion, conflict?)
2. Check upstream issues/discussions for context on this design decision
3. Based on findings: either file an upstream issue with our reasoning for the complementary model, or file issue + PR if the community seems receptive
4. We are contributors, not owners - this is a suggestion, not a demand

We work around it locally via the global PRIME.md override, so this is not urgent for us.
notes:
## Session Setup

When picking up this bead, this is an upstream investigation + community contribution task, not a local code change.

### Required Reading
1. `cmd/bd/prime.go` lines ~300-440 - the hardcoded fallback content
2. `internal/templates/agents/defaults/beads-section-minimal.md` - template for AGENTS.md injection
3. Our global PRIME.md at `$APPDATA/beads/PRIME.md` - the complementary model we use locally
4. Upstream beads repo issues/discussions for prior art on this topic

### Suggested Task Breakdown
- [ ] Search upstream repo (github.com/steveyegge/beads or gastownhall/beads) for issues mentioning TaskCreate, TodoWrite, or MEMORY.md
- [ ] Search for PRs or discussions about the prohibition language
- [ ] Check git blame on prime.go lines 310, 428 to find when/why the prohibition was added
- [ ] Determine: was this a deliberate design decision with reasoning, or a default that was never questioned?
- [ ] Draft an issue describing the complementary model: beads for cross-session strategic tracking, Tasks for in-session tactical execution, bd remember for project-scoped memory, MEMORY.md for cross-project user preferences
- [ ] If community seems receptive (based on existing discussions), also draft a PR changing the fallback to match the complementary model
- [ ] If there's strong upstream reasoning we haven't considered, document it and close this bead

### Context
- We work around this locally via global PRIME.md override, so this is P3 urgency for us
- Parent epic: bd-mh6 (Cross-project beads OS)
- The complementary model is already proven in our workflow and documented in our global PRIME.md
- Related: the beads-onboard skill also needs to teach this distinction
close_reason: Research complete: prohibition is deliberate, battle-tested design decision. PR #1289 (rsnodgrass, 2026-01-25) re-tightened after observing agents defaulting to TodoWrite over beads (issues #486, #499). MEMORY.md prohibition exists because bd remember stores in beads k/v store instead. PR #1353 tried to re-integrate TodoWrite but was withdrawn. Our local PRIME.md override with the complementary model is the right local workaround - no upstream change warranted.

title: Document shared Dolt server lifecycle for external tools (bt)
description: bt needs to understand how the shared Dolt server lifecycle works so it can reliably start the server when the port file exists but nobody's listening. Key questions: (1) Does bd dolt start always start the shared server, or can it start per-project? What controls which mode? (2) Does the port file (~/.beads/shared-server/dolt-server.port) get cleaned up when the server stops, or does it persist as stale? (3) Can shared and per-project servers coexist on different ports? (4) What's the correct invocation for an external tool to ensure the shared server is running? (5) How does this interact with writes - when bt shells out to bd for CRUD operations, does bd expect the server to already be running or does each command auto-start/stop?
notes:
## Dolt Shared Server Lifecycle Investigation

### Q1: Does bd dolt start always start the shared server, or can it start per-project?

Either mode. Controlled by ResolveServerMode() in internal/doltserver/servermode.go:60-96. Priority order:
- BEADS_DOLT_SERVER_MODE=1 env var → ServerModeExternal (suppress auto-start)
- BEADS_DOLT_SHARED_SERVER=1 env var or dolt.shared-server config → ServerModeExternal
- metadata.json dolt_mode: embedded → ServerModeEmbedded (legacy)
- metadata.json dolt_server_port → ServerModeExternal
- Default → ServerModeOwned (per-project, beads auto-manages)

Shared mode uses ~/.beads/shared-server/ (or $BEADS_SHARED_SERVER_DIR). Per-project mode uses the project's .beads/ directory. ResolveServerDir() (line 197-207) dispatches based on mode.

### Q2: Does the port file get cleaned up when server stops, or persist as stale?

Cleaned up on normal stop. Stop()/StopWithForce() both call cleanupStateFiles() (doltserver.go:955-968) which removes both PID and port files. On crash/kill, the port file may persist temporarily, but IsRunning() (line 482-510) detects dead PIDs and removes both stale files (line 502-503). Tests verify this at lifecycle_integration_test.go:135-140.

### Q3: Can shared and per-project servers coexist on different ports?

No, by design. ResolveServerMode() is absolute per beadsDir. A project is in one mode or the other. If BEADS_DOLT_SHARED_SERVER=1 is set globally, all projects use shared. reclaimPort() (line 314-347) checks port ownership against the Dolt data directory and errors if another project owns it.

### Q4: What's the correct invocation for an external tool to ensure the shared server is running?

Two approaches:

**Explicit start:**
BEADS_DOLT_SHARED_SERVER=1 bd dolt start

**Transparent auto-start:**
Just run any bd command. The storage layer (store.go:972-1010) calls doltserver.EnsureRunningDetailed() on TCP dial failure when cfg.AutoStart is true. For shared mode, default port is 3308 (DefaultSharedServerPort constant, line 89). Port discoverable from ~/.beads/shared-server/dolt-server.port.

### Q5: How does this interact with writes - auto-start/stop per command?

Auto-starts on demand, NEVER auto-stops. Server persists across commands. OpenConnection() (store.go:972) triggers EnsureRunningDetailed() on connection failure. Comment at line 980-982: 'leaving the server up avoids endpoint churn and circuit-breaker trips between commands'. Explicit stop requires bd dolt stop.

Auto-start can be suppressed via: BEADS_DOLT_AUTO_START=0, BEADS_TEST_MODE=1, ServerModeExternal, or dolt.auto-start: false in config (open.go:131-158).

### Implications for bt (beadstui)

bt should NOT try to manage the Dolt server lifecycle itself. Instead:
1. Set BEADS_DOLT_SHARED_SERVER=1 in the environment before shelling out to bd
2. Rely on bd's transparent auto-start - first bd command will start the server if needed
3. Read ~/.beads/shared-server/dolt-server.port to discover the port if bt needs direct DB access
4. If port file exists but server is dead, just run any bd command - IsRunning() will clean up stale state and auto-start will bring up a fresh server
5. Don't auto-stop - let the server persist for the session duration

### Key files
- internal/doltserver/doltserver.go — lifecycle (Start, Stop, IsRunning, EnsureRunningDetailed)
- internal/doltserver/servermode.go — mode resolution
- internal/storage/dolt/store.go:972-1010 — transparent auto-start trigger
- internal/storage/dolt/open.go:131-158 — auto-start config resolution
close_reason: Investigation complete. All 5 questions answered, findings recorded in notes. bt consumed findings and shipped the fix (startSharedDoltServer helper) in commit e1a27133. bt-zsy8 also closed.

title: Document shared Dolt server lifecycle for external tools (bt)
description: bt needs to reliably start/manage the shared Dolt server. Key questions that need answering: (1) Does bd dolt start always start the shared server, or can it start per-project? What controls which mode? (2) Does the port file (~/.beads/shared-server/dolt-server.port) get cleaned up when the server stops, or does it persist as stale? (3) Can shared and per-project servers coexist on different ports? (4) What's the correct invocation for an external tool (bt) to ensure the shared server is running? (5) How does this interact with the CRUD-from-TUI story where bt shells out to bd for writes? Context: bt currently finds the stale port file, tries to connect, fails, prints a warning, and falls back to starting a per-project server. This is wrong - bt should start the shared server instead.
notes: Cross-project: bt-zsy8 depends on this. bt's auto-global path (cmd/bt/root.go:211-226) needs to know the right way to start the shared server. Also informs the CRUD design.
close_reason: Recreating with matched ID bd-zsy8 to pair with bt-zsy8

title: Resubmit PR #3136 to upstream (closed due to fork pollution)
description: PR #3136 was closed because the git identity consolidation (Apr 10) rewrote history on the fork, polluting the PR with unrelated commits. Need to: 1) create a fresh branch from current upstream main, 2) cherry-pick just the relevant commits cleanly, 3) resubmit as a new PR. The original PR had contributor-quality description. From Apr 10 session brief: 'PR #3136 was closed due to fork pollution, requiring fresh submission'.
close_reason: Already resolved: maintainer extracted fix into PR #3168, merged 2026-04-10. No resubmission needed.

title: Research: does beads already expose cross-project bead linking or foreign bead filing?
description: Before building new features (bd-cxd cross-project filing, bd-k8b same-ID linking), check what beads upstream already supports. The shared Dolt server + bt global mode can already query across projects. Questions: can bd create reference issues in other project databases? Is there a foreign key or cross-database dep mechanism? Does bd dep add work across prefixes? Check the beads CLI surface (bd --help, bd dep --help) and the Dolt schema for cross-database references.
notes: This is a prerequisite for bd-cxd and bd-k8b. If the capability already exists, we just need convention/docs, not new features.
close_reason: Research complete. Upstream already has: (1) external: dep protocol with bd ship fulfillment, (2) cross-prefix dep routing via PR #3168, (3) shared server mode with per-project DB isolation, (4) full federation with peer sync. Gaps: no automatic external dep resolution at query time (bd ready doesn't check remote projects), no cross-database bare-ID deps outside shared server. The building blocks exist - bd-cxd and bd-k8b need convention/config, not new primitives.

title: Same-ID-different-prefix bead linking across projects
description: Design pattern: when beads-123 exists upstream and bt-123 is created locally, they share identity but have project-specific context. Use global dep relationship to link them. Enables cross-project sorting in bt global view and preserves both the canonical upstream issue and the local project-specific tracking. Need to decide: which direction does the dep go, is it bidirectional, or is it a new relationship type (not dep but 'mirrors' or 'corresponds-to').
close_reason: Migrated to bt project as bt-6cfg

title: Cross-project bead filing: sessions should create beads in other projects when findings are cross-related
description: Currently sessions only file beads in their own project. When a session discovers something relevant to another project (e.g. bt session finds updoots needs charm v2 scout report), there's no mechanism to file the bead where it belongs. Need: convention in prime.md instructing agents to cd into other projects and file beads there, plus provenance tracking (source session ID, source repo) so the target project knows it came from outside.
close_reason: Migrated to bt project as bt-k9mp

title: bd dep add fails for cross-prefix dependency targets despite storage layer support
description: The storage layer's isCrossPrefixDep() correctly skips target validation for cross-prefix deps (e.g., bt-ssk7 depends on bd-fk1), but the CLI's resolveIDWithRouting() in dep.go line 267 resolves the target first and fails with 'no issue found'. The external: prefix works as a workaround but bare cross-prefix IDs should work too - the storage layer was designed for it. Test: from a project with prefix 'bt', run 'bd dep add bt-xxx other-yyy' where other-yyy has a different prefix. Expected: dep stored with cross-prefix skip. Actual: resolution error before storage layer runs. Fix: when resolveIDWithRouting fails for the dep target AND the target has a different prefix than the source, skip resolution and pass the raw ID to AddDependency.

title: Cross-project beads: no raw SQL against shared server
description: Sessions observed using raw dolt --data-dir SQL to update issues across databases. Must use bd commands within project directories instead. Canonical bead in dotfiles repo. Blocked by bt-ssk7 (proper cross-database tooling).

title: Fix steveyegge references in claude-plugin layer for gastownhall migration
description:
The repo moved from steveyegge/beads to gastownhall/beads but ~15 references to steveyegge remain in the claude-plugin directory (plugin.json, commands, skills). This blocks Claude Code users from upgrading the plugin from 0.60.0 to 1.0.0 because the marketplace still points at the old repo.

Upstream issue: https://github.com/gastownhall/beads/issues/3059

Scope: ONLY the claude-plugin layer. The broader migration (go.mod, install scripts, docs) is tracked in the upstream issue and should be handled separately.
design:
PR scope limited to files under claude-plugin/ and .claude-plugin/:
- claude-plugin/.claude-plugin/plugin.json - update repository, homepage, author url
- claude-plugin/commands/version.md - update install script url
- claude-plugin/commands/quickstart.md - update github pages url
- claude-plugin/commands/workflow.md - update github repo link
- claude-plugin/skills/beads/README.md - update 3 github repo links
- claude-plugin/skills/beads/resources/TROUBLESHOOTING.md - update go install, issues link
- claude-plugin/skills/beads/resources/WORKTREES.md - update documentation links

Workflow: run local grep audit first to verify/extend findings from GitHub API search, update issue with any new findings, submit PR linking to issue, update issue with PR link.
notes:
Research conducted via GitHub API from dotfiles session on 2026-04-06. Agent searched specific files - a local grep -r steveyegge would be more thorough and may find additional refs.

The beads:import skill description says '(removed)' but the bd import command works fine - that's a separate stale description bug, not related to this migration work.
close_reason: PR gastownhall/beads#3060 submitted. 15 replacements across 7 files in claude-plugin/. 2 refs intentionally kept (author profile URL, go module path).

title: Brainstorm: Claude Code deep-dive for beads plugin integration
description:
Context: Claude Code evolves rapidly with new features. Beads plugin needs to leverage Claude Code capabilities effectively - Tasks, plan mode, memory, hooks, skills, subagents. Need comprehensive audit of current features and how they complement beads.

Scope: Research Claude Code CLI features, identify integration opportunities, understand how plan mode + Tasks + beads comments/fields work together for session lifecycle. Feeds into the revised /beads:onboard skill design.

Done-when: Brainstorm doc with Claude Code feature audit, beads integration map, and specific plugin skill recommendations.
notes: 2026-03-23: WS1 (PR #2654) and WS2 (PR #2664) both fix-merged to main by steveyegge. Onboard worktree rebased onto current main (v0.62.0). Close Reason Format was rejected from prime - steve says don't bootstrap conventions through prime, use validation.on-close to nudge instead. --by flag on supersede was actually --with. Main has moved significantly - embedded dolt, removed daemon infra, Gas Town renamed, command aliases landed (#2639/#2638). sms-li8 (onboard revision) still blocked by this brainstorm.

title: Revise /beads:onboard plugin skill with new architecture
description:
Motivation: Current onboard v3 skill teaches rich conventions but agents dont read the generated convention files. Tpane dogfooding proved inline AGENTS.md checklist works better than pointing to reference.md. Need to revise skill based on all brainstorm findings.

Scope: Interactive project-specific setup (labels, priority calibration, commit format, examples). Sets validation.on-create warn via bd config. Generates conventions files. Writes inline creation checklist in AGENTS.md. Session lifecycle teaching (plan mode + Tasks + beads comments). Memory systems complementarity (bd remember vs Claude Code memories).

Scope boundary: Claude Code plugin skill only. Output is tool-agnostic (AGENTS.md, .beads/conventions/). Does not modify Go CLI code.
design: Plugin first as proof of concept. Output designed to be tool-agnostic so it benefits all agents. If proven, convention generation logic can be ported to Go CLI later.
acceptance_criteria: Running /beads:onboard on a project produces: conventions files, AGENTS.md with inline checklist, validation config set to warn. Agents in subsequent sessions produce structured descriptions and close reasons without reading separate files.

title: Add bd doctor --conventions quality checks
description:
Motivation: Quality auditing currently requires running 6 separate commands (lint, stale, orphans, find-duplicates, dep cycles, label list) and mentally combining results. Tpane dogfooding required a manual hour-long audit that should be one command.

Scope: Add convention/quality checks to bd doctor following existing modular doctorCheck pattern. Runs: lint (missing sections by type), stale (forgotten beads), orphans (referenced in commits but open), label taxonomy drift (labels in use but not documented). Outputs unified report.

Scope boundary: Uses existing doctor extensibility. Not a new command - fits naturally as --conventions or --quality flag.
design: Doctor is the right home per codebase research - modular check pattern with doctorCheck struct, extensible via --check flag. Not a separate bd health command.
acceptance_criteria: bd doctor --conventions runs lint + stale + orphans + label drift checks in one pass. Follows existing doctorCheck struct pattern. Outputs actionable report.
close_reason:
Summary: Surfaced quality, lifecycle, and workflow commands in prime output, template, and doctor
Change: Added 4 new sections to prime CLI output, 2 sections to beads-section.md template, and doctor --check=conventions composite check
Files: cmd/bd/prime.go, internal/templates/agents/defaults/beads-section.md, cmd/bd/doctor.go, cmd/bd/doctor_conventions.go
Discovery: All planned commands (defer, supersede, human, formula, mol, stale, orphans, preflight) exist and work. gofmt issues in prime.go and doctor.go are preexisting.

title: Improve beads-section.md template with quality content
description:
Motivation: The full profile template (beads-section.md) is what non-hook agents see (Codex, Factory, Mux, OpenCode). Currently shows basic workflow and --reason Completed as example. Same quality teaching gap as prime output but for a different audience.

Scope: Mirror the prime improvements in the embedded template. Close reason template, field usage, description requirements by type. Flows to all full-profile integrations via existing hash-based staleness detection.

Scope boundary: Template body content only. No marker format changes, no profile changes, no architecture changes.
acceptance_criteria: beads-section.md includes close reason template, field usage, and description requirements. Hash updates trigger staleness detection for existing setups.
close_reason:
Summary: Surfaced quality, lifecycle, and workflow commands in prime output, template, and doctor
Change: Added 4 new sections to prime CLI output, 2 sections to beads-section.md template, and doctor --check=conventions composite check
Files: cmd/bd/prime.go, internal/templates/agents/defaults/beads-section.md, cmd/bd/doctor.go, cmd/bd/doctor_conventions.go
Discovery: All planned commands (defer, supersede, human, formula, mol, stale, orphans, preflight) exist and work. gofmt issues in prime.go and doctor.go are preexisting.

title: Improve bd prime output with quality teaching content
description:
Motivation: bd prime is the SSOT for agent workflow context, injected at every SessionStart and PreCompact. Currently teaches 8 commands and shows --reason Completed as an example. 90% of CLI features are invisible to agents. Dogfooding on tpane (85+ beads) showed 24% thin descriptions, --acceptance never used, --validate unknown.

Scope: Add close reason template (Summary/Change/Files/Discovery/Verify/Risk), field usage (--acceptance, --design, --notes, --validate), description requirements by type, lifecycle commands (bd defer, bd supersede, bd close --suggest-next), hygiene commands (bd lint, bd stale, bd orphans), bd human for human-in-the-loop, bd preflight before PRs. Must stay compact - prime is injected every session.

Scope boundary: Does NOT change prime architecture or override mechanism. Content improvements only.
design: Surfacing features IS respecting the desire paths philosophy. Agents cant use what they dont know exists. Not enforcing anything - teaching what the tool offers.
acceptance_criteria: Prime output includes close reason template, field usage table, description requirements by type, and surfaces bd lint, bd human, bd defer, bd preflight. Total output stays under 120 lines.
close_reason:
Summary: Surfaced quality, lifecycle, and workflow commands in prime CLI output
Change: Added Close Reason Format, Quality Tools, Lifecycle & Hygiene, and Structured Workflows sections to outputCLIContext()
Files: cmd/bd/prime.go, cmd/bd/testdata/prime_content.txt
Discovery: All planned commands verified to exist. MCP mode correctly unchanged.

title: Add validation.on-close config for close reason quality floor
description:
Motivation: 26% of close reasons across 704 beads are terse (<20 chars), mostly just 'Closed'. No enforcement mechanism exists. bd create has validation.on-create config (error/warn modes) but bd close has nothing equivalent.

Scope boundary: Warn mode only, defaulting to OFF. Not enforcing policy on other workflows. Aligns with Yegge philosophy of enabling quality as a choice. Onboard or setup can opt projects in via bd config set.
design: Warn-not-error by default. Quality is a choice (Yegge philosophy) but it cant be a choice if the tool doesnt surface it. Mirrors existing validation.on-create pattern.
acceptance_criteria: validation.on-close config exists with warn/error/off modes. Default off. Warn triggers on terse reasons. Does not block any workflow unless explicitly set to error.
close_reason: Already implemented upstream: close.go:67-69 reads validation.on-close config and calls ValidateCloseReason. template.go:116-125 has the validation logic.

title: Fix --validate to check --acceptance field (parity with bd lint)
description:
Observed: bd create --validate only checks description text for required section headings via substring match. If acceptance criteria is provided via the dedicated --acceptance flag, --validate does not see it.

Expected: --validate should check both description text AND the --acceptance field, matching bd lint behavior (LintIssue in internal/validation/template.go already does this per GH#2468).

Steps to reproduce:
1. In a real .beads project, run: bd create --validate --type=feature --title=x --description='Motivation: blah' --acceptance='tests pass' -p2
2. Observe: fails with 'missing Acceptance Criteria' because --validate only checks description text
3. bd lint on the same issue after creation would PASS because LintIssue checks the acceptance field

Root cause: ValidateTemplate() in template.go checks description only. LintIssue() has the fix (lines 80-112) but ValidateTemplate does not. Also --dry-run bypasses --validate entirely.
acceptance_criteria: --validate checks --acceptance field same as bd lint does. --dry-run no longer bypasses --validate.
close_reason: Already fixed upstream: create.go now calls LintIssue (not ValidateTemplate) with AcceptanceCriteria field populated. GH#2468 parity comment at line 245.

title: Feature request: beads audit slash command for quality gating
description:
## Why

Beads quality degrades over time as projects accumulate issues across sessions. Labels drift from taxonomy, descriptions get vague, duplicates accumulate, dependencies go unwired. Manual audits work but don't scale - a 97-issue audit took a full session of manual orchestration.

This is a feature request for an official `/beads-audit` or `bd audit` command that automates quality gating across any beads project.

## What

A command that audits all open issues against the project's quality conventions and produces an actionable report:

1. **Score** each issue against `.beads/conventions/` (description templates, label taxonomy, acceptance criteria)
2. **Report** with quality tiers (A=good, B=minor fix, C=needs rewrite, D=closeable)
3. **Identify** consolidation candidates, missing labels, missing deps, stale issues
4. **Optionally execute** bulk fixes with user approval (label updates, closures, consolidations)

### Key insights from dogfooding (bt project, 2026-04-14)
- `bd export` is the right data source - avoids N individual `bd show` calls
- Labels are the #1 quality gap in practice (0% taxonomy adoption found despite conventions existing)
- Consolidation must be additive (never lose context when merging beads)
- Interactive approval for destructive actions is essential
- Project-specific conventions must be read dynamically, not hardcoded

## Scope

This could live as:
- A `bd audit` subcommand in the CLI
- A slash command in the beads plugin for Claude Code
- Both (CLI produces report, slash command adds interactive execution)

## Acceptance Criteria

- Reads project-specific conventions from `.beads/conventions/`
- Scores all open issues and produces a structured report
- Identifies: missing labels, missing acceptance criteria, vague descriptions, duplicates, stale issues
- Does not auto-execute changes without explicit approval

## Paired
Paired: mkt-qa01 (dogfood implementation in marketplace)

title: bd export -o with /tmp path fails cross-tool on Windows
description:
## Problem

On Windows with MSYS/Git Bash, `bd export -o /tmp/file.jsonl` writes to a bash-specific temp mount (e.g. `C:\Users\sms\AppData\Local\Temp` or Git Bash's `/tmp`). But other tools (Python's `open("/tmp/file.jsonl")`) resolve `/tmp` literally and throw FileNotFoundError.

This is a Windows ecosystem issue more than a beads bug, but ergonomics could help.

## Discovered By

Agent feedback - had to `cp /tmp/file.jsonl ./file.jsonl` as workaround, then hit further piping issues with `cat file | python -c ...` due to path resolution.

## Proposed Solution

- `bd export` could default to writing to current directory (stdout or `./issues.jsonl`) instead of requiring `-o`
- Or use a platform-aware temp path

## Priority Rationale

P3 - minor ergonomics, easy workaround exists (use `-o ./file.jsonl`)

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Export has a sensible default output location that works cross-tool on Windows

title: Track upstream issue #3182: .claude/ gitignore inconsistency
description: Filed issue #3182 on the upstream beads repo about .claude/ directory gitignore behavior being inconsistent. This bead tracks it so we remember to follow up on the response. From Apr 10 git identity consolidation session.
close_reason: Upstream #3182 closed as COMPLETED on 2026-04-11. No follow-up needed.

title: External bead notification hook: alert project when outside session files a bead
description: When a session from repo A cd's into repo B and creates a bead, repo B should get a notification. Design ideas: repo author signing (session carries source identity), notification hook triggered on foreign-authored beads, visual distinction in bt TUI for externally-filed beads. Edge cases: permissions (can any session write to any project?), notification spam, source session gone. Related to cross-project bead filing feature.

title: Brainstorm: formulas and templates as development SOP encoding
description:
Context: Beads has an entire formula/molecule system (.formula.toml, protomolecules, molecules/wisps) that could encode development SOPs like TDD, spec-driven development, bug-fix workflows. Completely invisible to most users. Need to understand what exists today, whats possible, and whether pre-built formulas would improve agent workflow quality.

Scope: Research and brainstorm only. Understand formula system, identify useful SOP patterns, determine if this is a beads core improvement or separate initiative.

Done-when: Brainstorm doc at docs/brainstorms/ with findings on formula capabilities, proposed SOP formulas, and recommendation.

title: test lint warning
close_reason: test issue

title: Clean up stray unicode-named files in repo root
description: Untracked files with unicode characters in names (Date꞉, Related꞉, Status꞉, ꞊꞊For) sitting in the beads repo root. Likely artifacts from a macOS/Windows encoding issue. They clutter git status output. Investigate origin, then delete.
close_reason: Files already cleaned up - no unicode-named files found in repo root.

title: Test bead - verify setup
description: Quick round-trip test. Delete after confirming.
close_reason: Test complete - contributor setup verified

value: Onboard v4 architecture: 3 layers. Layer 1 = beads core (prime output + templates + bd health command). Layer 2 = bd onboard CLI command (tool-agnostic, interactive + flags). Layer 3 = Claude Code skill wrapper. Close reason needs Discovery field (institutional knowledge). --acceptance, --design, --notes, --validate all underutilized. 90% of CLI features invisible to agents. Brainstorm: docs/brainstorms/2026-03-16-layered-quality-architecture-brainstorm.md

On Windows bash, ~/.local/bin/bd (no .exe) takes priority over bd.exe. The Makefile only installs bd.exe, so after make install you must also copy bd.exe to bd: cp ~/.local/bin/bd.exe ~/.local/bin/bd. Otherwise the stale extension-less binary runs instead of the new one.

Beads Dolt DB for this repo is LOCAL ONLY - no remote configured. We are contributors to gastownhall/beads, not maintainers. The Dolt remote was removed 2026-04-13 because auto-push kept failing (no common ancestor with the GitHub fork's Dolt history). bd dolt push will error with 'remote origin not found' - this is expected and harmless. Do NOT re-add a remote or try to fix push errors. All beads data persists locally in the shared Dolt server at ~/.beads/shared-server/dolt/beads/. Skip 'bd dolt push' in session close protocol for this project.

Memory systems are complementary, not competing. bd remember = project knowledge (any tool, syncs via Dolt, shared with team). Claude Code memories = personal preferences and behavioral corrections (Claude-specific, personal). Current beads plugin instruction to avoid MEMORY.md is too aggressive - should clarify the split, not ban Claude memories. At scale with parallel agents, project memories matter more than preferences. In interactive sessions, both matter.

Local directory-source plugins (sms-marketplace) do NOT auto-sync their cache. CC copies source to ~/.claude/plugins/cache/sms-marketplace/<plugin>/<version>/ at install time and never refreshes. Editing source at ~/System/marketplace/ requires manual cache sync: Copy-Item -Recurse -Force source/* cache/. Then /reload-plugins. This was verified 2026-03-26 by editing guardrails SKILL.md + plan_audit_gate.py, restarting session, and diffing - cache stayed stale. Needs automation fix in .files repo.

On Windows, always use 'make build' (not bare 'go build'). The Makefile passes -tags gms_pure_go which activates pure-Go regex fallback, avoiding the ICU header dependency. CGO_ENABLED=0 works as a quick workaround but loses the embedded Dolt backend.

bt (beadstui) is a companion TUI for beads, built by sms at ~/System/tools/bt (github.com/seanmartinsmith/beadstui). It reads .beads/ Dolt storage directly (internal/datasource/dolt.go), not via Go module import. Currently at v0.13.0. When reviewing beads changes, flag anything that touches: storage schema (internal/storage/dolt/schema.go, embeddeddolt/schema/), query format (queries.go), .beads/ directory structure, or Dolt transaction semantics - these could break bt. The 44-commit pull on 2026-03-26 included major storage refactors (new issueops/, versioncontrolops/ packages, HOP column removal, RunInTransaction refactor) that should be checked against bt.

Community contributors working on same CLI discoverability problem: DreadPirateRobertz opened #2611 (command aliases, 166 failed attempts data), #2612 (flag aliases, --comment/--reason), PR #2639 (top-level aliases), PR #2638 (--format json alias). Our WS1/WS2 PRs are complementary - they make wrong guesses work, we surface the right commands upfront. maphew is a collaborator interested in the analysis methodology. Good to cross-reference and stay aware of overlapping work.

Cross-project beads OS ownership model (bd-mh6 epic): beads (bd) = engine (CLI, server lifecycle, data model, gates/human/formulas). bt = dashboard (TUI, global mode, visual orchestration). marketplace (mkt) = OS/teaching layer (plugins, skills, hooks, beads-onboard, PRIME.md, conventions). Key paired beads: bd-0il/mkt-0il (feature discovery), bt-zsy8/bd-zsy8 (server lifecycle). See bd show bd-mh6 for full epic.

Cross-project beads use paired IDs: same suffix, different prefix. Use bd create --id to set explicitly. Example: dotfiles-x08 / mkt-x08. Add Paired note. Documented in global CLAUDE.md under Cross-Project Awareness.

When setting up a new project with beads, always use bd init --shared-server to connect to the global Dolt server. This ensures database-per-project isolation and enables cross-project visibility in BT.

Never PR from fork main. Always create a feature branch from upstream main. PRing from main includes all fork divergence (.beads data, local configs). Learned when PR #3136 was closed - maintainer extracted fix into #3168 instead.

Dogfooding findings (tpane, 85+ beads): 24% thin descriptions, scope:* label drift (15+ undocumented), P0/P1 miscalibration, --acceptance never used, --validate unknown to agents, audit bead at P4 never surfaced via bd ready. Key fix: inline checklist in AGENTS.md > pointing to reference.md. Agents don't read separate files.

Plugin first as proof of concept, CLI later if it proves out. PRs go through seanmartinsmith GitHub account. Final position doc: docs/brainstorms/2026-03-16-final-architecture-position.md

bd create --validate IS implemented (not a no-op). Checks description for heading text: bug needs 'Steps to Reproduce' + 'Acceptance Criteria', feature/task need 'Acceptance Criteria', epic needs 'Success Criteria'. BUT --validate only checks description text, not --acceptance field. bd lint (LintIssue) IS smarter - checks --acceptance field too (GH#2468). Config: bd config set validation.on-create warn enables auto-validation. --dry-run bypasses --validate. Close reason has ZERO validation.

ALWAYS git fetch + check distance from upstream before starting any extensive work (planning, implementation, reviews). Upstream changes frequently affect design assumptions. This burned us on the dolt-native repo sync plan - the flock fix in 543e1943 directly affected our locking design.
